message:
test(fcp-conformance/session-replay): lock failed-verify non-poisoning + FIFO eviction (br-ti4ot)

Adds four conformance tests around the responder hello replay window:

- invalid_signature_does_not_burn_replay_nonce: a tampered Hello must
  fail with InvalidSignature and leave the (from, nonce) pair acceptable
  for a subsequent legitimate Hello with the same nonce. Otherwise an
  attacker that observes a fresh nonce could pre-emptively forge a Hello
  and lock out the legitimate initiator.
- timestamp_skew_rejection_does_not_burn_replay_nonce: stale Hellos are
  rejected with TimestampSkew and the nonce stays acceptable.
- attestation_node_mismatch_does_not_burn_replay_nonce: AttestationNodeMismatch
  rejection does not retain the nonce.
- evicted_hello_nonce_can_be_re_accepted_after_window_overflow: locks
  the documented bounded FIFO retention behaviour and verifies the
  most-recent two entries continue to be tracked after eviction.

All four call only public fcp_protocol::session helpers
(verify_hello_attested_with_replay, HelloReplayWindow::{new, check}) so
a regression in the production replay path fails conformance directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b","title":"Testing Completeness Program: non-mock acceptance coverage and high-signal E2E evidence","description":"Background:
- The repo already has meaningful testing assets: `crates/fcp-e2e`, `crates/fcp-host/tests/host_connector_integration.rs`, `fcp-testkit`, connector `tests/integration.rs`, and structured JSONL reporting.
- That is still not the same thing as full pure-unit coverage without mocks/fake peers or complete end-to-end acceptance coverage with operator-grade logs.
- The current 2026-03-23 inventory says the workspace has 149 connector directories and 28 core crates; 139/149 connectors show unit-test signal, 100/149 have crate-local `tests/`, 84/149 have `fcp-e2e` coverage, 0/149 expose connector-level `no_mock_integration.rs`, only 8 connectors document verification bundles, and logging/replay evidence exists for 92/149 connectors.
- The user question behind this program is therefore settled: no, the repo does not yet have full unit coverage without fake-backed confidence, and no, it does not yet have complete non-mock E2E scripts with great detailed logging across the workspace.

Program goal:
- Make pure-unit coverage explicit and truthful, with fake-backed suites demoted to `deterministic_contract` rather than silently counting as final unit evidence.
- Give every core crate and connector family an archetype-appropriate acceptance path: `local_non_mock`, `host_e2e`, `live`, or a documented combination.
- Make every E2E-capable suite emit durable, redacted, replayable artifacts that let future agents debug failures without rediscovering the whole test setup.

Definition of done for the overall program:
- The coverage matrix and acceptance taxonomy stay current and are enforced.
- Shared harnesses exist for HTTP, streaming/webhook/session, and DB/storage/queue/browser/local-process boundaries.
- Connector-family sweeps close both pure-unit floor gaps and non-mock acceptance gaps.
- Nightly/live suites have an explicit operating model for secrets, cost, cleanup, and artifact retention.
- CI and maintainer playbooks make the whole testing story executable rather than aspirational.","notes":"Clarification: repo policy still forbids real external-service calls in unit tests. This program treats "no mocks/fake stuff" as a requirement for acceptance layers (`local_non_mock`, `host_e2e`, `live`) while `pure_unit` stays focused on local logic and truthful suite classification.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:33.507188Z","created_by":"LilacLake","updated_at":"2026-03-25T00:34:42.461132Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T00:34:42.460968Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","quality","testing"]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.1","title":"Audit current workspace test surface and publish a connector/core coverage gap matrix","description":"Background:
- Before changing test infrastructure, we need one authoritative inventory of what exists today.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","title":"fcp.airtable: Attachment upload and download handling","description":"# fcp.airtable: Attachment Upload and Download Handling

## Goal
Handle Airtable attachments safely under strict sandboxing.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Scope
Operations (representative):
- `airtable.attachments.upload`
- `airtable.attachments.get` (fetch attachment metadata/URL)
- optional: `airtable.attachments.download` (if we choose to implement actual download)

## Key requirements
- Capability gated:
  - read vs write separated
- NetworkConstraints:
  - Airtable API host(s) allowed
  - attachment download hosts must be explicitly enumerated/pinned
  - validate URLs are HTTPS and match allowlist
- Data handling:
  - attachments move as **ObjectId references** (no local disk persistence)
  - enforce size limits and streaming bounds
- Logging:
  - never log attachment contents
  - URLs may contain opaque tokens; treat as sensitive

## Tests
- Allow/deny matrix for attachment URL hosts.
- Size limit and timeout tests.
- Redaction tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Attachment flows are safe-by-default and deterministic.
- Non-allowed hosts are blocked by Network Guard.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-g1s","title":"fcp.spotify: Search (Tracks, Albums, Artists, Podcasts)","description":"# fcp.spotify: Search (Tracks, Albums, Artists, Podcasts) (FCP2)

  - album
  - artist
  - playlist
  - show/podcast
  - episode
- Support filters:

- NetworkConstraints pinned to Spotify Web API host.
- Treat titles/descriptions as tainted external content.

  - `spotify.search`
  - `spotify.search.tracks`
  - `spotify.search.albums`
  - `spotify.search.artists`
  - `spotify.search.shows`
  - `spotify.search.episodes`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - market handling
  - rate limiting + retry/backoff

## Acceptance criteria
- Search results are stable and correctly typed.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-g2c","title":"fcp.airtable: Full CRUD operations on records","description":"# fcp.airtable: Full CRUD Operations on Records

## Goal
Implement record CRUD with batching, pagination, and strict safety controls.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Scope
Operations (representative):
- `airtable.records.list` (pagination, filter/sort)
- `airtable.records.get`
- `airtable.records.create` / `airtable.records.create_batch`
- `airtable.records.update` / `airtable.records.update_batch`
- `airtable.records.upsert`
- `airtable.records.delete` / `airtable.records.delete_batch` (Dangerous)

## Key requirements
- Capability gated:
  - read vs write vs delete are separate capabilities
- Rate limiting and backpressure are mechanical.
- Pagination:
  - support continuation tokens
  - bound page sizes
- Batch operations:
  - handle partial failures deterministically
  - stable error mapping per record
- Idempotency / retries:
  - upsert is preferred for retry-safe workflows
  - deletes and schema writes must be treated as Dangerous (policy + receipts)

## Tests
- Mock-only tests cover:
  - pagination edge cases
  - batch partial failure semantics
  - upsert conflict handling
  - redaction: never log record bodies by default

## Acceptance Criteria
- CRUD operations behave deterministically under retries.
- Dangerous operations are properly gated and audited.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","title":"[FCP3/P3.2] Define canonical FCPS durable object schemas for runtime truth","description":"Background: a mesh first platform needs durable forms for the facts that survive process death and justify later decisions. Receipts, checkpoints, lease state, revocations, connector runtime state, and operator explanations cannot remain host local conventions.

Scope: define canonical FCPS object schemas for durable state, including connector identity and state, receipts, checkpoints, lease and fencing records, revocation records, and explanation bundles. Design them for replay, repair, inspection, and failover rather than as opaque storage details. Provide deterministic object examples, snapshot formats, explanation bundle shapes, schema unit tests, replay integration tests, and detailed inspection logs that later conformance and end to end harnesses can diff and diagnose.

Acceptance: the platform has a durable object vocabulary for the facts it must remember, those facts no longer depend on one host memory image to exist, and the schemas are concrete enough for fixture driven validation, replay, operator inspection, and regression tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:05.315492Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-03-28T06:31:09.065699Z","closed_at":"2026-03-28T06:31:09.065467Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","fcps","mesh-first","phase-3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-2m2hl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:24.219178Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-d8jbq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:24.654535Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2081,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","author":"jemanuel","text":"WhiteCompass completed 2026-03-28: Published FCPS durable object schemas at docs/FCP3_FCPS_Durable_Object_Schemas.md.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-gif","title":"fcp.pinecone: Pinecone Vector Database Connector","description":"# fcp.pinecone - Pinecone Vector Database Connector (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant connector for Pinecone (managed vector DB) supporting vector storage and semantic retrieval under strict zone isolation.

## Manifest archetypes (closed set)
- `storage`
- `knowledge`

## Zone model
Single-zone bound: one connector instance ↔ one `ZoneId`.

## External surface
### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only the configured Pinecone endpoint(s) and port(s) required by declared operations.

### Credential injection
- API key via `CredentialId`.
- Never persist or log keys.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `pinecone.index.manage`
- `pinecone.vectors.read`
- `pinecone.vectors.write`
- `pinecone.search.query`

## Operations (initial)
- index: create/describe/list/delete
- vectors: upsert/fetch/delete/query

## Tests
- Mock-only unit tests with wiremock.
- E2E compliance run validates default deny + NetworkConstraints + receipts for writes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner.
- Deterministic tests (no live Pinecone calls).

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-k4ka","title":"fcp-graphql: GraphQL Client Infrastructure Library","description":"# fcp-graphql: GraphQL Client Infrastructure Library


## Goal
Provide a shared, typed GraphQL client library for connectors with retries/pagination/subscriptions, mapping errors to the FCP taxonomy and fully mock-tested.

## Overview
Shared GraphQL client library for connectors that interface with GraphQL APIs (Linear, GitHub, Notion, Shopify, etc.).

## Features

### Core Client
- Async GraphQL client with connection pooling
- Query/mutation/subscription support

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-k57","title":"fcp.jira: Bidirectional Beads sync integration","description":"# fcp.jira: Bidirectional Beads sync integration (FCP2)

## Goal
Implement **bidirectional synchronization** between Flywheel Beads issues and Jira issues for the connector’s bound zone.

This enables:
- Jira → Beads: convert incoming Jira work into native Beads (agent-ready tasks)
- Beads → Jira: propagate Beads updates back into Jira to keep humans in their existing workflows

## Non-goals
- “Backwards compatibility” with any older sync format or FCP1 behavior.
- Cross-zone sync from a single connector instance (still single-zone bound).

## Core requirements (hard)
- **Deterministic mapping** (same inputs produce same object updates).
- **Loop prevention** (Jira update triggers Beads update triggers Jira update… must stop).
- **Conflict detection** (concurrent edits) with explicit policy:
  - default: fail-closed + raise a conflict event
  - optional: last-write-wins only if explicitly enabled
- **Idempotency**: repeated webhook deliveries or retries must not duplicate issues/comments.

## Scope
### Mapping surface (minimum viable)
- Bead fields ↔ Jira fields:
  - title ↔ summary
  - description ↔ description (ADF/markdown conversion)
  - status ↔ workflow status
  - priority ↔ priority mapping
  - labels ↔ labels
  - assignee ↔ assignee
  - due date ↔ duedate
  - estimates ↔ timeoriginalestimate (if policy allows)
  - bead_id ↔ a dedicated custom field (or label) for stable correlation

### Sync directions
- `jira_to_beads`
- `beads_to_jira`
- `bidirectional`

### Event sources
- Jira webhooks (issue created/updated/commented/transitioned).
- Beads change events (local event stream from the platform).

## Connector state model (FCP2)
This bead MUST use the platform connector-state mechanism:
- Store a persistent mapping table:
  - `bead_id ↔ jira_issue_id/key`
  - last_seen_revision / fingerprint
  - last_sync_origin (jira|beads)
  - tombstones for deletions/archives
- Use singleton-writer lease semantics if the connector runs in multiple locations.

## Capability + approval model
Illustrative capabilities:
- `jira.issues.read` / `jira.issues.write`
- `jira.comments.write`
- `jira.webhooks.manage` (dangerous; provisioning only)

Policy guidance:
- Creating/transitioning issues in Jira is a write and must produce receipts/audit.
- Bulk sync operations may require an ApprovalToken depending on org policy.

## Deliverables
- A sync engine with:
  - field mapping layer
  - ADF ↔ markdown conversion
  - conflict detection + resolution policy
  - loop prevention via origin stamps + revision fingerprints
- Operation schemas:
  - `jira.sync.push_bead`
  - `jira.sync.pull_issue`
  - `jira.sync.reconcile`
- Structured logging:
  - correlation_id per sync run
  - per-item reason codes for skips/conflicts

## Test strategy (MUST)
### Unit/integration (mock-only)
- Webhook replay tests:
  - duplicate webhook deliveries do not duplicate Beads/Jira updates
- Conflict tests:
  - concurrent edits produce conflict events and do not silently overwrite
- Loop tests:
  - Beads update → Jira update → webhook does not re-trigger Beads update
- State tests:
  - mapping table persists across restarts
  - lease enforcement prevents double writers

### E2E compliance
- Use the shared compliance runner to confirm:
  - default deny on all sync operations
  - receipts/audit for all writes
  - NetworkConstraints enforcement

## Acceptance criteria
- A round-trip sync of a single issue converges in ≤2 sync cycles without loops.
- Conflict cases are explicit, logged, and surfaced as events.
- Sync behavior is deterministic and idempotent.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-rp0ej","title":"[MOR][HIGH] fcp-mesh invoke enforcement still permits unbound capability verification","description":"Reasoning mode: adversarial. Architectural context: the typestate split says operation execution must require instance-bound tokens, but MeshNode::enforce_invoke_request() still calls the deprecated CapabilityVerifier::verify() and explicitly accepts whichever verifier mode the caller constructed (crates/fcp-mesh/src/node.rs:920-944). In fcp-core, verify() simply runs verify_claims_inner and returns the ambiguous CryptographicallyVerified state (crates/fcp-core/src/capability.rs:1750-1768), while instance matching is only guaranteed by verify_bound() when self.instance_id is Some (crates/fcp-core/src/capability.rs:1786-1809). A caller that passes CapabilityVerifier::without_instance_binding() therefore bypasses instance_id enforcement entirely, because verify_claims_inner skips the match in that mode even when the token carries an INSTANCE_ID claim (crates/fcp-core/src/capability.rs:2003-2021). Suggested mitigation: make enforce_invoke_request require verify_bound()/BoundVerified or accept a BoundVerified token directly so mesh execution cannot run on an unbound verifier path.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:05:23.627698Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:39:37.544109Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:39:37.543566Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2838,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-rp0ej","author":"jemanuel","text":"Resolved in commit b5af8cdb.

Fix: swap enforce_invoke_request's deprecated verifier.verify(...) for verifier.verify_bound(...) at fcp-mesh/src/node.rs:953. Behavior:
- Properly-bound verifiers (CapabilityVerifier::new + instance_id): behavior unchanged — same 5 checks.
- Unbound verifiers (CapabilityVerifier::without_instance_binding): previously silently bypassed the instance check via verify_claims_inner's unbound branch; now yield FcpError::Internal at verify_bound which bubbles as MeshNodeEnforcementError::CapabilityVerification and fails invoke closed.

Unbound gateway-vantage verification remains valid but belongs at the gateway→connector handoff (verify_unbound + promote_with_instance), NOT at the terminal mesh invoke boundary.

Commit also includes a parallel agent's in-flight symbol_request zone-enforcement tightening (empty zones → fail closed) per AGENTS.md protocol for concurrent-agent work. Additional symbol_request / coordinator / transfer-state test failures under cargo test -p fcp-mesh --lib are attributable to that parallel work, not to this rp0ej invoke/capability change — enforce_invoke_request has no callers in the test suite.

— cod_4 / SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:39:37Z"}]}
